reasoning: China currently only operates one overseas military base (Djibouti). Establishing a new base takes several years of negotiations and construction. Among BRICS nations, only the UAE has been identified by US intelligence as a potential site for a Chinese military facility (Khalifa port), but construction was halted in 2021 under US pressure. Even if covert construction resumed, the UAE's strong security ties with the US make it highly unlikely they would allow a fully operational Chinese military base by the end of 2025. Furthermore, China historically avoids explicitly designating its overseas facilities as 'military bases.' Given these political and timeline constraints, the likelihood of an operational base being publicly announced by either China or the US IC in a BRICS country before 2026 is very low.
